test_cases.title: PDF invoice is automatically generated at the end of a billing period
preconditions: A user account exists with a valid subscription plan
The billing period for the user has just completed
steps: Allow a billing period to complete for the subscribed user
Navigate to the user's billing history section
Locate the most recently generated invoice
test_cases.expected_result: A PDF invoice is automatically present in the user's billing history immediately after the billing period ends, without any manual action required.

test_cases.title: Generated PDF invoice contains all required fields
preconditions: A billing period has completed for a user with a known name, billing address, plan name, and amount charged
An invoice has been automatically generated for that period
steps: Navigate to the user's billing history
Download the PDF invoice for the completed billing period
Open the downloaded PDF
Verify the presence and accuracy of: user's name, billing address, plan name, billing period start date, billing period end date, amount charged, and unique invoice number
test_cases.expected_result: The PDF contains exactly the user's name, billing address, plan name, billing period start and end dates, amount charged, and a unique invoice number, all matching the account and plan data on record.

test_cases.title: Each invoice is assigned a unique invoice number
preconditions: At least two billing periods have completed for one or more users
Invoices have been generated for each completed period
steps: Navigate to the billing history of a user with multiple completed billing periods
Record the invoice number from each generated invoice
Cross-check invoice numbers across multiple users if possible
test_cases.expected_result: No two invoices share the same invoice number; every invoice number is globally unique within the system.

test_cases.title: Invoice is available and downloadable as PDF from billing history
preconditions: A billing period has completed and an invoice has been generated
The user is authenticated and on their billing history page
steps: Log in as the user
Navigate to the billing history section
Locate the invoice for the completed billing period
Click the download option for the invoice
Verify the downloaded file format
test_cases.expected_result: The downloaded file is a valid PDF document (MIME type application/pdf) that can be opened and is not corrupted.

test_cases.title: User cannot access another user's invoice from billing history
preconditions: Two separate user accounts exist, each with at least one completed billing period and generated invoice
Both users are known by their invoice numbers
steps: Log in as User A
Attempt to access or download the invoice belonging to User B by manipulating the invoice identifier in the request (e.g., modifying URL or API parameter)
Observe the system response
test_cases.expected_result: The system denies access and returns an authorization error (e.g., HTTP 403 or equivalent), and User B's invoice data is not returned.

test_cases.title: No invoice is generated for a billing period that did not complete
preconditions: A user account exists with an active subscription
The current billing period is still in progress (not yet completed)
steps: Navigate to the user's billing history
Check for any invoice entries corresponding to the ongoing billing period
test_cases.expected_result: No invoice appears in the billing history for the in-progress billing period; only invoices for fully completed periods are listed.

assumptions: A 'completed billing period' means a period that has ended and for which payment processing has concluded.
The unique invoice number is globally unique across all users, not just per user.
The billing history is accessible to authenticated users only via a dedicated section in the application.
The system exposes an invoice identifier (e.g., in a URL or API parameter) that can be used to test unauthorized access.
PDF validity can be assessed by file format inspection (MIME type and file header) in addition to visual inspection.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) the exact timing or SLA for invoice generation after a billing period ends (immediate, within minutes, within hours); (2) whether invoices must be retained for a minimum period and any archival policy; (3) what happens if invoice generation fails — no retry, error notification, or fallback behavior is described; (4) whether the invoice PDF must meet any accessibility or locale/currency formatting standards; (5) the format or structure of the unique invoice number (sequential, UUID, etc.); (6) whether unauthenticated users or admin roles have any special access to invoices. These gaps may require additional test cases once clarified.
